info.title: Microsoft SARIF Multitool (includes SARIF SDK
info.description: Multi-purpose command line tool for analyzing and manipulating SARIF files

commands.name: absoluteuri
commands.description: Turn all relative Uris into absolute URIs (to be used after rebaseUri is run)

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
A directory to output the transformed files to.
If absent, each transformed file is written to
the same directory as the corresponding input
file.

options.name: --inline
options.description:
(Default: false) Overwrite each input file with
the corresponding transformed file.

options.name: --pretty-print
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce pretty-printed JSON
output rather than compact output (all white
space removed). If neither --pretty-print nor
--minify is specified, --pretty-print is set to
true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be
specified together.

options.name: --minify
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce compact JSON output
(all white space removed) rather than
pretty-printed output. If neither --pretty-print
nor --minify is specified, --pretty-print is set
to true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of output file
if it exists.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list, that should be
inserted into the log file. Valid values include
Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
VersionControlInformation, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list, that should be not be
persisted to or which should be removed from the
log file. Valid values include Hashes,
TextFiles, BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,

options.name: --uriBaseIds
options.description:
A key + value pair that defines a uriBaseId and
its corresponding local file path. E.g.,
SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version of the
output log file. Valid values are OneZeroZero
and Current

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --inline
options.description:
(Default: false) Write all newly generated
content to the input file.

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

options.description:

output rather than compact output (all white
space removed). If neither --pretty-print nor
--minify is specified, --pretty-print is set to
true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be
specified together.

nor --minify is specified, --pretty-print is set
to true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: inputFile
arguments.description: A path to a file to process.

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert a tool output log to SARIF format.

options.name: --tool
options.description:
Required. The tool format of the input file.
Must be one of: AndroidStudio, ClangAnalyzer,
CppCheck, ContrastSecurity, FlawFinderCsv,
Fortify, FortifyFpr, FxCop, PREfast, Pylint,
SemmleQL, StaticDriverVerifier, TSLint, or a
tool format for which a plugin assembly provides
the converter.

options.name: --plugin-assembly-path
options.description:
Path to plugin assembly containing converter
types.

options.name: --normalize-for-github
options.description:
Normalize converted output to conform to GitHub
Advanced Security code scanning ingestion
requirements.

options.name: --inline
options.description:
(Default: false) Write all newly generated
content to the input file.

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

arguments.name: inputFile

commands.name: export-validation-config
commands.description: Export validation rule options to an XML or JSON file that can be edited and used to configure subsequent analysis.

options.name: --format
options.description:
(Default: Json) The file format to persist settings to (if the
format cannot be inferred from the file name extension).

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Output path for exported analysis options

commands.name: export-validation-docs
commands.description: Export the documentation for the validation rules to a Markdown file.

options.name: --output-file-path
options.description:
Path to the generated Markdown file. Default:
Rules.md in the current directory.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

commands.name: export-validation-rules
commands.description: Export validation rules metadata to a SARIF or SonarQube XML file.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description:
Output path for exported analysis options. Use a
.json or .sarif extension to produce SARIF. Use .xml to
produce a SonarQube rule descriptor file.

commands.name: file-work-items
commands.description: Send SARIF results to a work item tracking system such as GitHub or Azure DevOps

options.name: --host-uri
options.description:
The absolute URI of the project in which the
work items are to be filed, for example
https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project} or

options.name: --split
options.description:
(Default: None) Apply a splitting strategy to
each input log file in order to break the file
into multiple bugs. Must be one of None, PerRun,
PerResult, PerRunPerRule, PerRunPerTargetPerRule
or PerRunPerTarget. By default ('None'), no
splitting strategy is applied (i.e. each input
file will be filed as a single bug).

options.name: --sync-workitem-metadata
options.description:
Enrich work item to be created with existing
work item metadata for any result workItemUri.

options.name: --file-unchanged
options.description:
File work items for both new and unchanged
baseline state results. If an unchanged result
already has an associated work item, a new work
item will not be created.

options.name: --configuration
options.description:
A path to an XML configuration file that will be
used to drive work item creation.

options.name: --no-validate
options.description:
Do not validate the SARIF log file before
filing.

options.name: --inline
options.description:
(Default: false) Write all newly generated
content to the input file.

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

options.name: --pretty-print
options.description:

commands.name: match-results-forward
commands.description: Track results run over run by persisting IDs and then matching them forward

options.name: --previous
options.description:
Path to a SARIF log containing the previous set
of results with result matching annotations on a
target (or empty if no previous log exists)

options.name: --output-file-path
options.description:
File path to output the annotated SARIF log with
result matching information.  Defaults to
<currentFile>-annotated.sarif

options.name: --pretty-print
options.description:

options.name: --version

arguments.name: currentFiles
arguments.description:
Path(s) to SARIF log(s) comprising the
current set of results, without result matching
information

commands.name: merge
commands.description: Merge multiple SARIF files into one.

options.name: --output-file
options.description:
(Default: merged.sarif) File name to write
merged content to.

options.name: --merge-empty-logs
options.description:
Merge log files with no results into the final
log.

options.name: --split
options.description:
(Default: None) Apply a splitting strategy to
the merged file. Must be one of None or PerRule.
By default ('None'), no splitting strategy is
applied (i.e. all input files will be merged
into a single log).

options.name: --merge-runs
options.description:
Merge runs of the same tool + verion combination
(requires eliding run-specific details such as
invocations data.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --version

commands.name: page
commands.description: Extract a subset of results from a source SARIF file.

options.name: --run-index
options.description: (Default: 0) 0-based index of the run to copy.

options.name: --index
options.description: Required. 0-based index of first result to include.

options.name: --count
options.description: Required. Number of results from index to include.

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of output file if it
exists.

options.name: --output
options.description: Required. A file path of the SARIF subset output file.

options.name: --map-ratio
options.description:
(Default: 0.01) Target map size relative to file size
(0.01 is 1%)

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: inputFile
arguments.description: A path to a SARIF file to process.

commands.name: query
commands.description: Find the matching subset of a SARIF file and output it or log it.

options.name: --expression
options.description:
Required. Result Expression to Evaluate
(ex: (BaselineState != 'Unchanged'))

options.name: --write-to-console
options.description:
(Default: false) Whether to write
matching results to the console

options.name: --non-zero-exit-code-if-count-over
options.description:
(Default: -1) Whether to return a
non-zero exit code if the count exceeds
a threshold

options.name: --return-count
options.description:
(Default: false) Exit Code is the count
of matching results

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of
output file if it exists.

options.name: --pretty-print
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce pretty-printed
JSON output rather than compact form.

options.name: --output
options.description:
A file path of the SARIF subset output
file, if desired.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: inputFile
arguments.description:
A path to a SARIF file to
process.

commands.name: rebaseuri
commands.description: Rebase the URIs in one or more sarif files.

options.name: --base-path-value
options.description:
Required. Base path value to use while rebasing
all paths.  E.x. 'C:\bld\1234\bin\'

options.name: --base-path-token
options.description:
Required. Variable to use for the base path
token (e.x. 'SRCROOT')

options.name: --rebase-relative-uris
options.description:
(Default: false) All relative uris will be
rebased to the base-path-value if true. Default
is false.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory

file.

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version of the
output log file. Valid values are OneZeroZero
and Current

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: inputFile
arguments.description: A path to a file to process.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validate a SARIF file against the schema and against additional correctness rules.

options.name: --json-schema
options.description: Path to the SARIF JSON schema.

options.name: --update-inputs-to-current-sarif
options.description:
Update any SARIF v1 or prerelease v2 files
to the current SARIF v2 format.

options.name: --output
options.description:
File path to which analysis output will be
written.

options.name: --verbose
options.description:
Emit verbose output. The resulting
comprehensive report is designed to
provide appropriate evidence for
compliance scenarios.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
Recurse into subdirectories when
evaluating file specifier arguments.

options.name: --config
options.description:
Path to policy file that will be used to
configure analysis. This defaults to
'default.configuration.xml' beside the
main tool; passing value of 'default' or
removing that file will configure the tool
to use its built-in settings.

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Do not log results to the console.

options.name: --statistics
options.description:
Generate timing and other statistics for
analysis session.

options.name: --hashes
options.description:
Output MD5, SHA1, and SHA-256 hash of
analysis targets when emitting SARIF
reports.

options.name: --environment
options.description:
Log machine environment details of run to
output file. WARNING: This option records
potentially sensitive information (such as
all environment variable values) to any
emitted log.

options.name: --plug-in
options.description:
Path to plug-in that will be invoked
against all targets in the analysis set.

options.name: --invocation-properties
options.description:
Properties of the Invocation object to
log. NOTE: StartTime and EndTime are
always logged.

options.name: --rich-return-code
options.description:
Emit a 'rich' return code consisting of a
bitfield of conditions (as opposed to 0 or
1 indicating success or failure.

options.name: --optimize
options.description:
Omit redundant properties, producing a
smaller but non-human-readable log.

options.name: --pretty-print
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce pretty-printed
JSON output rather than compact output
(all white space removed). If neither
--pretty-print nor --minify is specified,
--pretty-print is set to true.
--pretty-print and --minify cannot be
specified together.

options.name: --minify
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce compact JSON
output (all white space removed) rather
than pretty-printed output. If neither

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of output
file if it exists.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list, that should be
inserted into the log file. Valid values
include Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
VersionControlInformation, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
not be persisted to or which should be
removed from the log file. Valid values

options.name: --uriBaseIds
options.description:
A key + value pair that defines a
uriBaseId and its corresponding local file
path. E.g., SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version of
the output log file. Valid values are
OneZeroZero and Current

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description:
One or more specifiers to a file,
directory, or filter pattern that resolves
to one or more binaries to analyze.
